title: add/copy --chown cannot determine username
body:
1. add/copy --chown can work with uid

```
# grep buildah /etc/passwd
buildah:x:1001:1001:Test buildah:/home/buildah:/bin/bash

# buildah add --chown 1001:1001 nginxc auth.json 
```
2. add/copy --chown fail to determine username, the user `buildah` is existing.
```
# buildah add --chown buildah nginxc auth.json 
error adding content to container "nginxc": error determining run uid: user: unknown user error looking up user "buildah"
# buildah add --chown buildah:buildah nginxc auth.json 
error adding content to container "nginxc": error determining run uid: user: unknown user error looking up user "buildah"
```

comments.body: The buildah user needs to be defined inside of the container image.  It is not looking at the hosts /etc/passwd, attempt the same tests with "bin" rather then buildah and it will work, because bin is defined inside the container image /etc/passwd.
The uid 1001 is not in the image's /etc/passwd as well, why it can work?

If the USERNAME is a Int, it will not attempt to look for the content in the /etc/passwd file in the container but will just use the UID.  The problem is when using a non UID, the container runtime needs to translate the UserName into a UID via the /etc/passwd in the container.  This works the same way on Docker.

@rhatdan from what I understand this is not a bug but an expected behavior.
Can we close it?
